key.name: open-history
key.description: Keybinding that opens the clipboard history popup.

key.name: history-size
key.description: Maximum number of entries kept in the clipboard history.

key.name: auto-paste
key.description: Automatically simulate Ctrl+V after selecting an entry from the popup.

key.name: capture-images
key.description: Whether image content copied to the clipboard is stored in history.

key.name: capture-files
key.description: Whether files copied in a file manager (e.g. Nautilus) are stored in history.

key.name: capture-formats
key.description: Store every format an app publishes (HTML, RTF, embedded objects) so pasting into a document keeps tables, styles and colours. Plain-text targets are unaffected.

key.name: flavor-max-bytes
key.description: Formats larger than this are dropped rather than truncated. The item's own content is never subject to this limit.

key.name: entry-max-bytes
key.summary: Maximum size of extra formats per item
key.description: Total budget for the extra formats of a single history item.

key.name: exclude-passwords
key.description: Skip clipboard content flagged as a password by the source application.

key.name: rich-text-wm-classes
key.description: Windows whose WM class or app id contains any of these tokens (case-insensitive) receive the formatted representation (HTML or RTF) of an item instead of plain text.

key.name: terminal-wm-classes
key.description: Windows whose WM class or app id contains any of these tokens (case-insensitive) are pasted into with Ctrl+Shift+V instead of Ctrl+V.
